digest.top_line: REVELUTION: +68.9 mm³ more coronary plaque at 12mo with leuprolide vs relugolix in men on ADT + pelvic RT · relugolix may spare the heart.
digest.tldr: Single prostate signal today: in men on ≥6mo ADT with definitive pelvic RT, relugolix produced less coronary plaque progression than leuprolide (adjusted 68.9 mm³ difference, NCPV-driven), a mechanistic correlate for HERO's MACE benefit. Surrogate imaging endpoint, N=94, 12-mo f/u · hypothesis-generating for ADT-agent choice when RT mandates androgen suppression.

sites.intro: Cardiovascular safety, not oncologic efficacy: the day's only prostate readout compares two ADT agents' coronary impact in men already committed to pelvic RT.

studies.name: REVELUTION
studies.tldr: Adjusted 12-mo coronary plaque volume rose 68.9 mm³ more with leuprolide vs relugolix in men on ADT with pelvic RT.
details: 📊 1° EP: 12-mo change in total coronary plaque volume (serial CCTA, HeartFlow automated quantification)
📊 Crude 12-mo total plaque change: +56 mm³ leuprolide vs +25 mm³ relugolix
📐 Adjusted between-arm difference 68.9 mm³ more total plaque with leuprolide (ANCOVA: age, statin use, baseline plaque)

subdetails: Total-plaque difference driven mainly by non-calcified plaque (NCPV)
Calcified plaque: no significant between-arm difference
Low-attenuation plaque: no significant between-arm difference, low absolute change

details: 🔍 Single-institution open-label RCT, 94 men, intermediate/high-risk non-metastatic prostate ca (June 2020-2024)
🔍 ADT pts randomized 1:1: relugolix (360mg load → 120mg/d) vs leuprolide 3-mo depot, ≥6mo ADT
🔍 All pts got pelvic RT ± nodes; ADT agent was the only randomized variable (CCTA reads blinded, stratified by ASCVD 10-yr risk)
🔗 Mechanistic correlate for HERO (2020), which showed lower MACE with relugolix vs leuprolide

subdetails: Surrogate imaging endpoint (plaque volume), not clinical MACE
No p-values or CIs for the total-plaque difference reported in source
RT-alone (no-ADT) control arm was a parallel non-randomized prospective cohort

verdict.rationale: Randomised, prespecified plaque endpoint met and consistent with HERO's relugolix MACE benefit; surrogate imaging endpoint at single-institution N=94 reinforces emerging practice rather than changing it.
verdict.audience: Non-metastatic intermediate/high-risk prostate ca, on ADT with pelvic RT

open_questions: Does the plaque-volume difference translate to clinical MACE reduction?

related_trials.brief_title: Effects of Relugolix vs Leuprolide on Cardiac Function in Patients With Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial compares the effect of relugolix to leuprolide on cardiac function and performance in patients with prostate cancer. Androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) has been a key component for the treatment of advanced prostate cancer for decades. The term androgen deprivation therapy means lowering a man's testosterone. Long-term studies show that ADT may contribute to a detriment to cardiac health and predisposes men to developing cardiac diseases. Recent studies suggest that men…

related_trials.brief_title: REVELUTION-2: Relugolix+Abiraterone Acetate (AA) Versus Leuprolide+AA Cardiac Trial
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase III/IV trial compares the impact of leuprolide and abiraterone acetate (AA) versus relugolix and AA on the heart in hormone-naive patients with advanced prostate cancer receiving pelvic radiation therapy. Leuprolide is in a class of medications called gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonists (GNRHa). It prevents the body from making luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone (LHRH) and luteinizing hormone (LH). This causes the testicles to stop making testosterone (a male hormone) in men…
